topic: Someone finds an unsigned birthday card in their mailbox
raw_output:
[story 1]

The mail slot clattered, a sound that seemed impossibly loud in the otherwise silent apartment. I shuffled to the door, my breath catching in my throat as I pulled out the contents. There, nestled amongst the junk flyers and bills, was a thick, cream-colored card. It was completely blank on the front, just a crisp white expanse. My fingers, cold and clammy, traced the edges. A prickling sensation crawled up my arms. Who would leave something like this? A strange, unsettling feeling settled deep in my chest, making my heart thump against my ribs like a trapped bird. I quickly shoved it into my pocket, desperate to get away from the mailbox, the silence of the hallway suddenly feeling heavy and watchful.

[story 2]

It was a Tuesday, and the usual monotony of the morning was shattered by the sight of it. An envelope, slightly thicker than the others, sat on the welcome mat. Inside, a card. Unaddressed, unsigned. The paper felt oddly textured under my fingertips, and a wave of unease washed over me, making my stomach clench tight. I stared at the stark white surface, my gaze darting around the living room as if expecting something to materialize from the shadows. Every small sound—the distant traffic, the hum of the refrigerator—seemed amplified, pressing in on me. I snatched the card up, my hands trembling visibly, and retreated to the kitchen, needing the solid reassurance of a countertop beneath my palms.

[story 3]

I didn't mean to look, but the card had caught my eye. It was tucked into the slot, an anomaly in the predictable flow of correspondence. As I retrieved it, a sudden tension seized my shoulders, pulling my back into an uncomfortable arch. The lack of any inscription, the sheer anonymity of it, sent a ripple of apprehension through me. My gaze flickered from the card to the darkened corner of the hallway, lingering there for a beat too long. I clutched the paper tightly, the edges digging into my palm, and tried to move with a deliberate slowness, acutely aware of every creak in the floorboards, convinced that whatever this object represented was observing my every move.
